title: Zephyranthes pantanalensis
intro: Zephyranthes pantanalensis, also known as the Pantanal Zephyr Lily, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, native to the Pantanal region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Zephyranthes pantanalensis include alkaloids, such as pantanaline and pantanalidine, as well as saponins and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Zephyranthes pantanalensis is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory infections and other health issues.
taxonomy: Zephyranthes pantanalensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. It belongs to the genus Zephyranthes and is a type of rain lily. The plant is native to the Pantanal region of South America. The classification of Zephyranthes pantanalensis places it in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Liliopsida, order Asparagales, family Amaryllidaceae, genus Zephyranthes, and species Z. pantanalensis. Further classification may involve infraspecific ranks, but its primary classification is within the Amaryllidaceae family.
